description_long: This triumphal arch is a monumental stone structure featuring three large, evenly spaced archways supported by robust columns. The central arch is flanked by two identical side arches, all framed by a detailed entablature and bordered by thick, block-like piers. The surface is textured to resemble cut stone blocks, giving it a sense of historical gravitas and permanence. The columns are simple and unadorned, with square bases, and the overall design is symmetrical and austere, lacking ornate decorative reliefs or sculptures. The top of the arch is flat, with no visible roof or attic story, and the structure appears to be solid and heavy, typical of classical Roman architecture. The color palette is dominated by shades of gray, enhancing the impression of weathered stone.
description: A monumental stone triumphal arch with three large archways, robust columns, and a textured gray stone finish. The design is symmetrical, austere, and reminiscent of classical Roman architecture.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing three large archways, robust columns, and detailed stone block texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow depth of the structure and the solid, block-like profile.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the three archways and stone texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow depth and solid profile.
